Maintain parallel local-GAAP and group-GAAP accounting books for each entity

features.name: Group Structure & Ownership

features.description: Translate foreign subsidiary statements to group reporting currency using configurable methods and rates.

features.name: Minority Interest
features.description: Calculate and account for non-controlling interest in consolidated equity and earnings.

features.name: Acquisition Accounting

capabilities.name: Revenue Recognition (ASC 606 / IFRS 15)
capabilities.description: Apply the five-step ASC 606 / IFRS 15 model — identify contract, performance obligations, transaction price, allocation, and recognition — for businesses selling bundled, subscription, or multi-element arrangements. Critical for SaaS and professional services.

requirements.text: Maintain a contract master data repository that captures contract terms, identified performance obligations, and their characteristics (distinct goods/services, timing, etc.)

Build and maintain a standalone selling price (SSP) library with methods for determining SSP (observable market price, adjusted market assessment, expected cost plus margin) for all distinct performance obligations
Automatically allocate transaction price to identified performance obligations using SSP-based proportional allocation or other systematic methods
proxy_refs: Automatic allocation of transaction price across obligations

requirements.text: Define and apply recognition schedules for each performance obligation, distinguishing between point-in-time recognition (upon delivery/transfer of control) and over-time recognition (based on progress toward satisfaction)
proxy_refs: Recognition schedule (point in time vs over time)

requirements.text: Track deferred revenue and contract liabilities, automatically recognizing revenue as performance obligations are satisfied
Handle contract modifications (scope changes, price adjustments) and determine whether they create new contracts or amendments to existing contracts
Estimate and constrain variable consideration (discounts, rebates, penalties, contingent fees) using the most likely amount or expected value method, with periodic reassessment
Generate ASC 606 / IFRS 15 disclosure reports including contract assets, contract liabilities, performance obligations, and revenue recognition policies
Provide revenue waterfall and backlog reporting to track recognized revenue, deferred amounts, and future revenue potential by contract, customer, or business segment

features.name: Contract Management
features.description: Capture and maintain contract terms, performance obligations, and contract modifications.

features.name: Price Allocation
features.description: Determine standalone selling prices and allocate transaction price to performance obligations.

features.name: Revenue Recognition
features.description: Define recognition schedules and automatically recognize revenue as performance obligations are satisfied.

features.name: Variable Consideration
features.description: Estimate, constrain, and reassess variable consideration such as discounts, rebates, and contingent fees.

features.name: Reporting & Analytics
features.description: Generate ASC 606 / IFRS 15 disclosures, revenue waterfalls, and backlog reporting.

capabilities.name: Data Security, Privacy & Consent
capabilities.description: Finance data is the most sensitive category in any business — PDPA consent, encryption at rest and in transit, retention policies, audit trails, and secure destruction. Universal — PSG requires every pre-approved vendor to meet PDPA compliance, Cyber Essentials Mark, and VA/PT audits regardless of which module they cover.

requirements.text: Capture and maintain audit trail for all PDPA consent records related to personal data processing

requirements.text: Support deletion and anonymization of customer and employee personal data upon request

requirements.text: Encrypt financial data at rest using industry-standard encryption algorithms
proxy_refs: Encrypt financial data at rest and in transit

requirements.text: Encrypt financial data in transit using TLS or equivalent secure protocols

requirements.text: Log and audit all record-level access to financial data with user identity, timestamp, and action details

capabilities.name: User Accounts, Roles & Access Control
capabilities.description: Multiple staff use the finance system, each with their own login and role-based permissions — AR clerk, AP clerk, controller, CFO, auditor — with segregation of duties built into the role matrix. Universal — PSG requires access control as part of its Cyber Essentials baseline.
